title: Blueprint Signal Canvas
prompt: Create a production-ready Blueprint Signal Canvas using TypeScript React, Tailwind CSS, and Framer Motion. This original ambient background pattern is a pale measurement grid with selectable signal nodes, designed to sit behind content without reducing readability. Build a #F4F5F6 Bento stage with a white 22px-radius inset panel, #111111 labels, #4A4A4F metadata, a #ECECEE grid, and only cyan and violet node accents. Never use a card gradient. Place a small semantic toolbar above the canvas that toggles between Sparse and Dense signal layouts, and describe the current layout through an aria-live polite label. Use real buttons with aria-pressed, visible focus rings, 44px targets, and a concise text legend so color is not the sole cue. On layout change, let nodes move with a spring around stiffness 280 damping 25 while labels fade over 180ms; prefer transform and opacity. The calm resting state must be informative in server rendering. Do not use actual canvas APIs, random values, remote assets, network calls, browser storage, or timers. The component should fit a compact card at 320px and grow naturally in a detail preview. Keep contrast AA, shadows soft, and decorative grid lines hidden from screen readers. For prefers-reduced-motion, snap nodes to their final position and only fade the status text. Return complete client component code, then a pure CSS approximation with suggested HTML.
